response:
I've read `ISSUE-41.md` and made the call. Decision record written to `docs/decisions/0035-tiered-agent-do-phase-defer-adoption.md` (id 0035, next after 0034, 5,748 chars — under the 6,000 cap):

**Decision: do not adopt tiered-agent execution yet — greenlight running the pre-registered test instead.**

1. **No adoption on assertion.** No `benchmarks/*tiered*` run exists. ADR 0024 already forbids shipping a Do-phase redesign on plausibility alone, however good the cost case in the issue reads.
2. **Test is cleared to run now.** The issue's stated dependency ("after the current skills/agents benchmark batch," ISSUE-41.md:4) is closed — its last tracked item, issue #30, closed in `c82a21e`/#97.
3. **Resolved the three "owner calls before spend" blocking execution (Comment 3):**
   - *Grader independence* — source the blind grader from a model family outside all three tiers under test (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus), rather than settling for the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual ADR 0019 would otherwise force on a 3-tier design. Flagged as `[unverifiable]` with a REOPEN-IF fallback if that model can't actually be invoked as an `agent()` tier.
   - *Hermetic controls* — user-CLAUDE.md relocation carries over; tool-deny doesn't (impossible for a tool-using task) — drop it and disclose the gap, don't silently omit it.
   - *Retry cap / feedback format* — cap stays at the pre-registered ≤3; validator→worker feedback carries only the task + failing checks, never the grading expectations (preserves the no-teaching-to-the-test rule).
4. **Adopt/reject stays governed by the issue's own pre-registered rule** (delta ≥ -0.05 AND tokens/wall-clock ≤ 0.7× Sonnet-solo). This ADR unblocks the test, it doesn't pre-decide the outcome.

One caveat: I couldn't execute `node pdca-workflow/scripts/adr-lint.mjs docs/decisions` in this session (blocked in this sandbox) — I manually checked the file against the lint rules (frontmatter complete, no version strings, all `ADR NNNN` cites resolve, falsifiable criterion present), but please run adr-lint before merging per the sacred-rules requirement.

artifacts.docs/decisions/0035-tiered-agent-do-phas:
---
id: 0035
title: "Issue #41 tiered-agent Do-phase: defer adoption, greenlight the pre-registered test"
status: accepted
summary: "Do NOT adopt tiered-agent execution (Sonnet plans/validates, Haiku implements) for the pdca-workflow Do phase on assertion — no run exists yet. Greenlight the pre-registered test (ISSUE-41.md Comments 1/3) now that its stated batch dependency is closed; resolve the three owner-calls blocking spend so the script can actually ship. Adopt/reject stays governed by the issue's own pre-registered decision rule, not this ADR."
---

# 0035 — tiered-agent Do-phase: defer adoption, greenlight the test

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: none (owner-directed decision, made directly per explicit authorization; no advisor fan-out run)
- Context: ISSUE-41.md proposes a tiered agent hierarchy (top model plans+validates, cheap model
  implements) for the plugin's Do phase, "for AFTER the current skills/agents benchmark batch"
  (ISSUE-41.md:4). That batch's last tracked item (skill-description trigger A/B, issue #30) closed
  in `c82a21e` (#97); no `benchmarks/*tiered*` directory exists (checked: none). ADR 0024 already
  forbids adopting a context-cost artifact "on assertion" (0024 Decision 1); the issue's own
  Comment 1 pre-registers the exact non-inferiority test needed. Comment 3 leaves three "owner
  calls before spend" open, which is what's actually blocking execution.

## Decision
1. **Do not adopt tiered-agent execution now.** No empirical run exists; per ADR 0024, an
   unmeasured cost-saving redesign of the Do phase does not ship on plausibility alone, however
   compelling the cost case (ISSUE-41.md:5-9) reads.
2. **Greenlight running the pre-registered test** (ISSUE-41.md Comments 1 & 3) now — the stated
   dependency ("after the current skills/agents benchmark batch", ISSUE-41.md:4/20-21) is closed.
3. **Resolve the three owner calls (Comment 3, ISSUE-41.md:73) that block spend:**
   a. **Grader independence** — source the blind grader from a model family outside all three
      tiers under test (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus), rather than accepting the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet
      residual ADR 0019 would otherwise force onto this 3-tier design.
   b. **Hermetic controls** — user-CLAUDE.md relocation carries over unchanged; tool-deny does
      not (impossible for a tool-using implementation task) — drop it and disclose the residual
      in the writeup, don't silently omit the control.
   c. **Retry cap / feedback format** — retry cap stays at the pre-registered <=3
      (ISSUE-41.md:37); validator→worker feedback carries the task + the specific failing checks
      only, never the grading expectations (preserves Comment 1's "no teaching to the test").
4. **Adopt/reject itself is NOT decided here** — it stays governed by the issue's own
   pre-registered rule (delta >= -0.05 AND tokens/wall-clock <= 0.7x Sonnet-solo, ISSUE-41.md:51).
   This ADR unblocks the test; it does not pre-empt its outcome.

## Justification
Cost x risk x value: the Do phase is the plugin's core execution loop — silently defaulting it to
an unmeasured cheaper tier risks degrading every future `/decide` build step for a token saving
that was never confirmed. The test itself is cheap relative to that blast radius (~250-300 agent
invocations, ISSUE-41.md:71) and already fully designed (Comments 1 & 3) — the only real cost left
is resolving the three owner-calls, which this ADR does.

## Assumptions
- [verified] no prior tiered-execution benchmark run exists (`benchmarks/` listing has no
  `*tiered*` entry) — nothing to dedupe against before spending on a new run.
- [checkable-doc] the batch ISSUE-41.md names as its dependency is closed — its last known open
  item, issue #30, closed in `c82a21e` ("Trim all 4 skill descriptions per the measured trigger
  A/B (closes #30)", #97); no later commit reopens or adds to that batch.
- [unverifiable] a model family distinct from Haiku/Sonnet/Opus (available in this environment) is
  invocable as an `agent()` `model:` value inside a Workflow script — REOPEN-IF it is not: fall
  back to the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual per ADR 0019's existing precedent, and note it as a
  residual in the test writeup rather than silently substituting it.

## Rejected alternatives
- **Adopt tiered execution now on the idea's plausibility** — exactly what ADR 0024 exists to
  prevent; the cost case (ISSUE-41.md:5-9) is a hypothesis, not a result.
- **Defer the whole issue further** (don't run the test yet) — its stated dependency is closed,
  the design is fully pre-registered, and ADR 0006 already carries a REOPEN-IF pending this
  issue's result ("pending the issue #41 tiered-agent A/B", `docs/decisions/0006-*.md`) — further
  delay has a live opportunity cost and no new information favors waiting.
- **Accept the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual outright** rather than sourcing an independent grader
  — rejected while a genuinely independent grader is available at no added design cost; settling
  for the weaker residual when independence is cheap would be its own muda.

## Revisit triggers
- The test runs and its pre-registered decision rule resolves adopt/reject (ISSUE-41.md:51) —
  rationalize this ADR in place to record the outcome; don't spawn a new ADR for it.
- The independent-grader assumption is refuted (model unusable as an `agent()` tier) — fall back
  per the REOPEN-IF above before the test ships.

## Act (post-ship — 2026-07-14)
- [process] batch-dependency check done by commit inspection (no issue-tracker read access this
  session); re-verify against the actual issue tracker before the test's PR opens, in case #30
  was reopened or the batch grew a late item after this ADR.
